programmes.show_description: Joyce has been invited to a conference in Barcelona. It seems like a welcome break - until she finds out that she has to make a speech in Spanish. Can Mateo help her pronunciation? S9 E2
A chance meeting in a club takes Arthur into the world of fine art as he sees an opportunity to make some money by selling forgeries. S3 E3: Rembrandt Doesn't Live Here Anymore
When suspected woodworm is discovered in the upstairs flat, the girls and Robin have to find alternative accommodation while George calls in his so-called handyman. S4 E3: All In The Game
David and Amy Pearce arrive in Spain for their 'second honeymoon' using David's redundancy pay. S1 E1: Viva Espana
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
Hit 70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S4, E2: A Young Man's Fancy
Hit 70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S4, E3: When You've Got To Go
The Ravenglass and Eskdale Railway is a narrow-gauge steam line which runs up the Eskdale valley in the Lake District. It's a beautiful and secluded corner of England with a historic past.

A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
When Stan loses his uniform outside a cemetery's gates, he has to use his holiday money to buy a new one. S2 E6: Bon Voyage
Young Mr Grace has died and the Grace Brothers department store has closed when the former staff attend a reading of his will and find that they have inherited a hotel. S1 E1: Grace & Favour
The former staff of Grace Brothers are settling into their new roles after their first night in their own hotel, but they need money to get the hotel up and running. S1 E2: Grace & Favour
Mrs Slocombe finds herself in court for stealing a horse-drawn cart, and Mr Humphries' relationship with Mavis Moulterd continues to develop. S1 E3: Grace & Favour
A party of Americans stay at the Millstone Country Hotel for a weekend - it means £1,000 plus tips, so the staff set about bringing the ageing hotel up to scratch. S1 E4: Grace & Favour
When Ruth is depressed following the 'deception' of a recent male friend, Rigsby takes what he believes to be his chance to woo her. S2 E1: The Permissive Society

Miss Jones is collecting for a famine charity when Philip challenges Rigsby to go 48 hours without eating - Rigsby gladly accepts the offer but soon finds his will weakening. S2 E2: Food Glorious Food
Health and fitness are the new buzzwords in the house when Rigsby's keenness to portray himself in prime condition leads him to challenge Philip to a boxing match. S2 E3: A Body Like Mine
When Miss Jones becomes engaged to a librarian and looks set to move out, Rigsby is distraught but is determined that no other man should have her. S2 E4: Moonlight and Roses
Rigsby has found a new lodger called Seymour who is staying in town away from his country estate, but Philip and Alan aren't quite as convinced of his authenticity. S2 E5: A Perfect Gentleman
Rigsby is hoping to take new tenant Brenda out for the evening, but he has a cashflow problem and a string of unwelcome visitors who start arriving at the house. S2 E6: Last of the Big Spenders
Could rumours of the Grey Lady who haunts the house be true? Rigsby certainly believes so, but he has not seen her yet... S2 E7: Things That Go Bump In The Night
Classic sitcom about a miserly landlord. Rigsby faces the prospect of a lonely Christmas until Alan and Philip arrive unexpectedly. S2 E8: For the Man Who Has Everything (Christmas Special 1975)
When Albert lands a large windfall on the premium bonds and soon goes on a wild spending spree, Harold soon goes from bitter to jealous. S3 E6: The Bonds That Bind Us
To bring in some extra cash, Albert decides to take in a lodger; Harold is none too happy and moves out of the family home and finds himself another job. Will he ever come back? S3 E7: The Lodger
Harold is finally tying the knot but is jilted at the altar, leaving the bridegroom humiliated and in a terrible state; however, the best man is far from upset. S4 E1: And Afterwards At...
Harold picks up a rare antique figurine on his rounds bearing the well-known crossed swords; thinking he's found an item worth selling, he is determined to make a large profit. S4 E2: Crossed Swords
Harold thinks the family home needs central heating; Albert is delighted until he hears that his son plans to install it all himself. S4 E3: Those Magnificent Men and Their Heating Machines
The bills are piling up but there's no money to pay for them; as the creditors and bailiffs arrive in the yard, Albert and Harold decide to take drastic action. S4 E4: The Siege of Steptoe Street
One of Arthur's business interests is being targeted - Terry gets involved to find out what is going on, but is Arthur's business partner the problem? S1 E8: Come in T-64, Your Time Is Ticking Away
Ray quickly steps into his shifty uncle's shoes when Arthur allows himself to be persuaded into retirement.
When The Solana's meat supplier lets Joyce down, she asks Lesley to help her out - who in turn enlists Mateo. The Dawsons, meanwhile, worry about Jodie's upcoming audition. S9 E3
The Gallagher unit is rocked when an unexpected visit from social services catches them off guard and causes the family to fall apart. S6 E13
Robin accompanies Chrissy to her nephew's christening while Jo runs out of excuses not to hear George sing in the British Legion's choir concert. S4 E5: The Tender Trap
Rigsby becomes convinced that magic is real after Philip bangs a ritual spear on the floor - while Ruth tries to offer herself to Rigsby, Philip and Alan play a trick on him. S1 E2: Black Magic
When The Solana's meat supplier lets Joyce down, she asks Lesley to help her out - who in turn enlists Mateo. The Dawsons, meanwhile, worry about Jodie's upcoming audition. S9 E3
When Mad Micky goes on the run to protest his innocence, Terry has to look after him and Arthur sees his chance of a scoop. S3 E4: Looking For Micky
With the aid of a jacket full of battle ribbons and Else's chair, Alf and Arthur find themselves in the money. S2 E5
Larry gives girls fake names; the most recent one he uses is Robin Tripp, so Chrissy and Jo think Robin is a real bounder when a girl turns up saying she is pregnant. S4 E4: Never Give Your Real Name
The two couples go to see a bullfight to see El Macho in action - David is fascinated, but the fight doesn't live up to his expectations. S1 E2: El Macho
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
Hit 70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S4, E4: When Love Walks In
Hit 70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S4, E5: An Open-And-Shut Case
The train journey along the West Cumbrian coast leaving Barrow-in-Furness, past the nuclear plant at Sellafield and taking us to Whitehaven has some stunning scenery which belies its industrial past.

Arthur digs a large hole for himself when he recruits some ex-cons as cheap labour for a landscape gardening enterprise amid a spate of local burglaries. S6 E1: Give Us This Day Arthur Daley's Bread
The terrible news about Lady Marjorie begins to affect everybody at Eaton Place - as Richard waits for news, an expected visitor calls late at night shocking all downstairs. S3 E2: A House Divided
Aveline's wedding is on again, Shifty starts a greengrocery business from his hearse and Mrs Boswell's new friendship brings a few surprises. S5 E12
The Steptoes go for their weekly visit to the cinema; unfortunately, their tastes in big screen entertainment differ wildly. S3 E5: Sunday For Seven Days
The staff are up early to greet a new arrival at Hatley Station - another arrival later in the day is less welcome... S1 E7: Past Love
When Miss Jones sees a prowler through her window, Rigsby suspects everyone else as a police detective appears and begins to investigate. S1 E6: The Prowler
When Chrissy's mother comes to stay, Jo and Chrissy try to hide the fact that their new flatmate is a man. S1 E2: And Mother Makes Four
The coast is clear for Gary to have a holiday with Phoebe, since Yvonne has packed her bags for a trip to Korea. But Gary also has to deal with an unexpected visitor. S4 E3: Out of Town
Jack looks after a socialist lecturer who is speaking at the Co-Op Hall, and Billy takes Bill to a New Year's party at Jessie's against his will. S1 E11: Happy New Year, Some Say
Arthur digs a large hole for himself when he recruits some ex-cons as cheap labour for a landscape gardening enterprise amid a spate of local burglaries. S6 E1: Give Us This Day Arthur Daley's Bread
Bittersweet sitcom. Penny and Vince go to a wedding as Daphne is subjected to a goodwill visit from the Pinner clan. S2 E4: Caught on a Shoulder Strap
Chrissy is practising for her driving test and is receiving lessons from Robin; meanwhile, George has started making home brew liquor which turns out stronger than expected. S4 E2: One for the Road
Classic sitcom featuring the accident-prone Frank Spencer and his long-suffering wife. Frank's attempt at building Jessica a doll's house in woodwork class gets out of hand. S3 E2: Wendy House
When Tracey has enough of Dorien entertaining gentlemen callers and Sharon not bringing in a living wage, she throws them both out and they end up in Sharon's old bedsit. S12 E4: Going, Going, Gone
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
A sophisticated antiques dealer turns up at the yard and Harold is very impressed, but Albert thinks he's after more than antiques. S5 E3: Any Old Iron
Mr Spooner annoys the household by playing his radio loudly - Rigsby is annoyed, but he finds it difficult to pluck up the courage to confront him about it. S1 E5: All Our Yesterdays
According to Bob and Shakespeare, 'wine is a gentle stimulant and good companion' - and so is Terry on the night Bob gets breathalysed. S2 E4: One for the Road
With typical aplomb, Arthur announces the launch of his Daley Into Space business, to flog some satellite dishes.
While Loretta takes Jodie back to the UK for her advert shoot, Rob tries to make use of her 5-star room, but the rest of the family have their own plans for its use. S9 E4

Yvonne gets a shock when she's confronted by a ghost from her past, and the consequences aren't worth thinking about. Desperate to keep the truth from Chesney and Meena, Yvonne must think fast. S6 E14
George has been claiming for a non-existent son for the past 19 years in order to fiddle his tax returns, but he is in danger of being caught when a tax inspector visits. S4 E6: My Son, My Son
Rigsby is dressed to kill as he takes Miss Jones out to dinner to celebrate her birthday. S1 E3: A Night Out
